The strongest STR candidates are detached homes with Bow River views or direct pathway access, particularly those on the escarpment sections that capture both river and mountain views. The community's three-landscape geography means properties vary significantly in character — floodplain homes have trail immersion while escarpment homes have views. Confirm the specific landscape position of any investment property before committing to a rate and occupancy forecast.

renter_intel.title: Three-Landscape Setting Produces Distinct Listing Tiers
renter_intel.description: Riverbend's escarpment properties with Rocky Mountain and river valley views command premium nightly rates and superior listing photography versus floodplain properties with trail immersion but no panoramic views. Investors should identify which landscape type their target property sits in before underwriting nightly rate assumptions — the performance delta between tiers is material.

renter_intel.title: City-Wide Pathway Connectivity via Bow River
renter_intel.description: The Bow River pathway at Riverbend's eastern boundary connects guests to a continuous off-road trail network reaching downtown Calgary, the Calgary Zoo, Inglewood, and the full south river corridor. Active guests — cyclists, runners, and walkers — find Riverbend among the most pathway-accessible suburban communities in Calgary, and this is a strong conversion point for the active-travel booking segment.

renter_intel.title: Glenmore Trail and Deerfoot Quick City Access
renter_intel.description: The intersection of Glenmore Trail and Deerfoot Trail immediately accessible from Riverbend gives guests one of the fastest drive times to downtown, CF Chinook Centre, and Calgary International Airport of any southeast community. This rapid access story converts well for corporate travellers who need city mobility but prefer quiet suburban accommodation.

whats_nearby.category: Carburn Park and Bow River Pathway
whats_nearby.items: Carburn Park — 135 hectares of natural environment park along the Bow River — provides wildlife, fishing, trails, and Bow River access within the community's immediate reach. The park connects to the city-wide pathway network, allowing guests to cycle or walk along the river all the way to downtown Calgary without a road crossing.

Fish Creek Provincial Park is accessible from Riverbend's southern edge via Deerfoot Trail or the Bow River pathway system, adding 100+ km of trails, Sikome Lake, and the Bow Valley Ranch visitor facilities to the community's outdoor recreation profile. The dual park access — Fish Creek to the south and Carburn/Bow River to the east — is exceptional among southeast communities.

whats_nearby.category: CF Chinook Centre and Glenmore Trail Commercial Corridor
whats_nearby.items: CF Chinook Centre is approximately 5 km west on Glenmore Trail, providing 250+ stores, major department stores, restaurants, and cinema within a 10-minute drive. The Glenmore Trail corridor also includes a range of restaurants, banks, and service retail that provide everyday guest convenience without requiring downtown travel.

riverbend.peak_season: June–August (Fish Creek Provincial Park proximity, Bow River access, mountain gateway).
riverbend.best_property_type: Detached 3-4 bed near Fish Creek Provincial Park or the Bow River.
riverbend.guest_profile: Outdoor enthusiasts, mountain-bound families, Bow River cyclists.
riverbend.stand_out: Detached homes near Fish Creek Provincial Park or the Bow River pathway.
riverbend.distinctive_fact: Riverbend sits along the Bow River with Fish Creek Provincial Park access — properties with river or park access see stronger bookings from outdoor recreation guests. BNBCalc data for this SE zone shows consistent summer performance.

groceries.name: Calgary Co-op Quarry Park Food Centre

homestead.name: Homestead
homestead.subtitle: New-Build NE Calgary with Airport Access and Wetland Greenspace
homestead.str_intro:
Homestead is one of Calgary's fastest-growing northeast communities, developed from 2021 onward along McKnight Boulevard and 80th Avenue NE. The master-planned neighbourhood sits roughly 15 minutes from Calgary International Airport (YYC), making it a natural fit for airline crew accommodations, extended airport layovers, and visiting families who prefer a residential setting over a hotel. Calgary Transit Route 80 launched in March 2025, providing a direct 25-minute loop to Saddletowne C-Train Station, which opens onward connections across the city. The community is anchored by a 19-acre environmental wetland, 4 km of walking pathways, and Homestead Landing — a 4-acre neighbourhood commercial hub under development featuring grocery, dining, medical, and childcare services.

Life in Homestead balances suburban calm with genuine urban connectivity. Residents reach CrossIron Mills (one of Canada's largest retail centres), Sunridge Mall, East Hills Shopping Centre (Costco, Cineplex), and the Genesis Centre / YMCA Saddletowne all within a 15-minute drive. Prairie Winds Park to the west offers a year-round outdoor amenity hub with a spray park, zipline, picnic areas, and tennis courts. Healthcare is accessible at Peter Lougheed Centre, a major regional hospital on the NE side of the city. The community's new housing stock — front-drive, laned, and paired homes — photographs exceptionally well and consistently draws strong interest from families and multi-generational groups seeking modern, well-appointed spaces.

Homestead's STR appeal centres on newer 3- to 4-bedroom detached homes in the $490K–$700K range, built by award-winning builders including Partners Development Group, Excel Homes, Trico Homes, and Genesis Builds. Many properties feature walk-out basements, 3-car garage options, and sites backing onto greenspace, which are strong selling points in listing photography. The separate-entry suite stock is mixed; investors should prioritise front-drive detached homes with basement suite potential or existing legal suites. Airport proximity sustains year-round demand with a modest summer occupancy bump; the Jan–Feb shoulder is the softest window. Verify any developer-covenant STR restrictions before listing, as some HOA agreements in new communities restrict short-term rental activity.

Prairie Winds Park to the west offers a year-round outdoor amenity hub with a spray park, zipline, picnic areas, and tennis courts. Verify any developer-covenant STR restrictions before listing, as some HOA agreements in new communities restrict short-term rental activity.

renter_intel.title: Airport Proximity — 15-Minute Drive to YYC
renter_intel.description: Homestead sits within a 15-minute drive of Calgary International Airport (YYC) via Stoney Trail and McKnight Boulevard. This is the community's single strongest demand driver, attracting airline crews on layover, early-morning departures, and inbound travellers who want a full home rather than a hotel room. Whole-home listings consistently outperform shared-room options for this segment.

renter_intel.title: Calgary Transit Route 80 — New Weekday Service
renter_intel.description: Calgary Transit's Route 80 (Homestead) launched in March 2025, operating a 24-stop circular loop every 30 minutes on weekdays from Saddletowne Terminal, which connects to the Blue Line C-Train. Car-free guests can now reach Marlborough, Franklin, and downtown stations without a vehicle. This materially widens the potential guest pool beyond airport-adjacent travellers.

renter_intel.title: New-Build Stock Photographs Exceptionally Well
renter_intel.description: Homestead's housing inventory is almost entirely new construction built from 2021 onward, with contemporary open-concept layouts, quartz countertops, and fresh finishes that generate strong click-through rates on listing platforms. Properties backing onto the wetland or community greenspace command a premium in listing photography and support higher nightly rate positioning within the $145–$250 range.

whats_nearby.items: The Genesis Centre is a major community and sports hub less than 10 minutes from Homestead. It houses two NHL-sized arenas, a fitness centre, gymnasium, and extensive programming for all ages. It doubles as a major event venue for NE Calgary community sports tournaments, which drive periodic demand spikes for nearby accommodation.

whats_nearby.category: East Hills Shopping Centre (Costco, Cineplex, Big-Box) — 12 min
whats_nearby.items: East Hills Shopping Centre on 68th Street NE is the primary retail and entertainment node for this area of the city, anchored by Costco, Cineplex Cinemas, Walmart, and a broad range of food and retail options. Its proximity means guests can stock full kitchens on arrival — a significant value-add for week-long family stays.

whats_nearby.category: 19-Acre Homestead Wetland and Pathway System — Onsite
whats_nearby.items: The community's own 19-acre environmental wetland reserve features a sheltered gazebo, boardwalks, and 4 km of paved pathways connecting into Calgary's regional pathway network. Year-round access to this natural corridor gives outdoor-oriented guests a meaningful amenity without leaving the neighbourhood. A cricket pitch and soccer fields are also planned for the Homestead community association site.

homestead.peak_season: Year-round stable (airport proximity). Slight summer bump.
homestead.best_property_type: Newer detached 3-4 bed within 15 minutes of YYC airport.
homestead.guest_profile: Airline crews on layover, airport travellers, visiting families.
homestead.stand_out: Newer 3-4 bed detached homes within 15 minutes of YYC airport.
homestead.distinctive_fact: Homestead is one of Calgary's newest NE communities, with new-build stock that photographs well and airport proximity. BNBCalc data for newer NE communities shows consistent performance for well-run whole homes.

braeside.name: Braeside
braeside.subtitle: Established SW Calgary Base Camp for Spruce Meadows and Mountain Getaways
braeside.str_intro:
Braeside is a mature southwest Calgary community established in 1965, bounded by Southland Drive to the north, 14 Street SW to the east, Anderson Road to the south, and 24 Street SW to the west. It sits on Macleod Trail SW, the principal south-Calgary arterial, placing Spruce Meadows — one of the world's premier equestrian and show-jumping venues — just minutes south via the Stoney Trail ring road at 194 Avenue SW. This access pattern makes Braeside a practical base for the equestrian event circuit, which draws international attendees during spring, summer, and fall tournament seasons. Downtown Calgary is roughly a 20-minute commute via 14 Street or the Anderson C-Train station nearby.

The Braeside Community Association, active since 1965 and headquartered at 11024 Braeside Drive SW, organises a year-round calendar of events including seasonal skating, youth programs, and the annual Stampede Breakfast. Residents draw on recreational amenities at both Fish Creek Provincial Park to the south (walking, cycling, and natural area access) and the Glenmore Reservoir system to the north, which offers rowing, canoeing, sailing, and 16 km of uninterrupted multi-use pathways. Southland Leisure Centre, a City of Calgary indoor aquatic and fitness facility, is accessible within the neighbourhood boundary. Southcentre Mall anchors retail access to the east on Anderson Road.

Braeside's housing stock is predominantly 1970s single-detached homes (averaging 1974 construction), with 56% of dwellings being single detached and a mix of row houses and apartments making up the remainder. Average assessed property values sit at $516,612 — approximately 33% below the city-wide average — meaning entry-point acquisition costs are favourable for STR investors. Detached 3-bedroom homes near Macleod Trail perform best as STR inventory, targeting Spruce Meadows event attendees in spring through fall and corporate Macleod Trail corridor travellers year-round. The slow season runs November through February; Stampede week in July provides a secondary revenue spike. Separate entry suite stock is mixed — confirm suite legality before listing.

Southland Leisure Centre, a City of Calgary indoor aquatic and fitness facility, is accessible within the neighbourhood boundary. Southcentre Mall anchors retail access to the east on Anderson Road.

renter_intel.title: Spruce Meadows Tournament Calendar — Genuine Demand Spikes
renter_intel.description: Spruce Meadows hosts internationally ranked show-jumping tournaments from May through September, including the National, Continental, and Masters events, each drawing 50,000+ spectators. Properties in Braeside with a 10–15 minute drive to the venue via Macleod Trail and Stoney Trail can market directly to event attendees, officials, and media crews who prefer whole-home accommodation over limited local hotel inventory.

renter_intel.title: Anderson C-Train Station — 5 Minutes
renter_intel.description: The Anderson LRT station (Red Line) sits just east of Braeside's boundary, providing direct rail access to downtown Calgary in under 30 minutes without a vehicle. This transit link is meaningful for corporate travellers, conference guests, and families who want downtown access from a quieter residential setting. Bus connections from Southland Drive feed into the broader transit network.

renter_intel.title: Mountain Gateway Positioning — 45 Minutes to Kananaskis
renter_intel.description: Braeside's Macleod Trail and Stoney Trail ring-road access puts Kananaskis Country within 45 minutes and Banff National Park under 90 minutes by car. Summer guests using the property as a mountain gateway base benefit from the quick south exit via Stoney Trail rather than navigating through central Calgary. This positions Braeside listings well for the June–September outdoor recreation traveller segment.

whats_nearby.category: Spruce Meadows — 10 min via Macleod Trail / Stoney Trail
whats_nearby.items: Accessed at 18011 Spruce Meadows Way SW via Macleod Trail South and 194 Avenue SW, Spruce Meadows is one of the world's great equestrian sport facilities. Beyond show jumping, it hosts Cavalry FC (Canadian Premier League soccer) home matches and seasonal markets. The grounds span 500+ acres and are open to the public on event days.

One of North America's largest urban provincial parks, Fish Creek Provincial Park borders Braeside to the south along Anderson Road. The park contains over 80 km of trails for hiking, cycling, and horseback riding, plus the Bow River, Sikome Lake, and numerous picnic and natural environment sites. A major year-round amenity for nature-oriented guests.

whats_nearby.category: Southcentre Mall / Southland Leisure Centre — 5–8 min
whats_nearby.items: Southcentre Mall on Anderson Road east of the community provides full retail and dining access, anchored by major department stores and restaurants. Southland Leisure Centre, a City of Calgary facility within the neighbourhood area, offers an indoor pool, fitness centre, and skating rinks — useful for families and guests seeking active indoor recreation during Calgary's shoulder and winter months.

braeside.peak_season: June–August (Spruce Meadows proximity, mountain gateway). July Stampede secondary.
braeside.guest_profile: Spruce Meadows event attendees, mountain-bound families, corporate travellers.
braeside.stand_out: Detached homes near Macleod Trail with easy Spruce Meadows access.
braeside.distinctive_fact: Braeside sits on Macleod Trail with direct Spruce Meadows access — BNBCalc comparables in this zone show whole homes achieving consistent summer performance. The Spruce Meadows equestrian events create genuine demand spikes.

cedarbrae.name: Cedarbrae
cedarbrae.subtitle: Established SW Calgary with Dual Access to Glenmore Reservoir and Spruce Meadows
cedarbrae.str_intro:
Cedarbrae is a mature southwest Calgary community established in 1973, bounded by Southland Drive to the north, 24 Street SW to the east, Anderson Road to the south, and Tsuut'ina Trail (the completed Southwest Ring Road) to the west. The community sits within easy reach of two major demand drivers: the Glenmore Reservoir and South Glenmore Park to the north, and Spruce Meadows to the south via Stoney Trail. The Heritage Station C-Train stop on 24 Street SW provides direct rail access to downtown Calgary, while Southland Drive connects quickly to Macleod Trail and the broader south corridor. Average assessed property values sit at $569,863 — approximately 26% below the city average — supporting entry-point STR acquisition.

Cedarbrae's tree-lined streets and mature 1970s–1980s architecture give it a settled, residential character that resonates with guests seeking a quiet suburban base. The Glenmore Reservoir's 16 km multi-use pathway system — accessible via South Glenmore Park north of the community — connects Cedarbrae to Heritage Park Historical Village, the Glenmore Sailing Club, the Weaselhead Flats natural area, and the Earl Grey Golf Club. Southland Leisure Centre is a city-run aquatic and fitness hub close by on Southland Drive. Cedarbrae Community Centre at 11024 Cedarbrae Drive SW hosts active programming including yoga, fitness classes, dance, and year-round community events including Winterfest and the annual Stampede Hoedown.

The housing stock is primarily single-detached 1970s–1980s homes, with 56% single detached and a mix of row houses and apartments. The southwest corner near Anderson Road is lower-value; best STR inventory is found on quieter residential streets toward the Glenmore Reservoir perimeter, where proximity to greenspace and the sailing club provides a lifestyle angle. Detached 3-bedroom homes near Macleod Trail or Tsuut'ina Trail access points are the strongest performers for corporate travellers and Spruce Meadows event guests. The November–February slow season is the main softness; July Stampede and summer Spruce Meadows events provide demand spikes. Suite stock is mixed — confirm legality before listing.

The Glenmore Reservoir's 16 km multi-use pathway system — accessible via South Glenmore Park north of the community — connects Cedarbrae to Heritage Park Historical Village, the Glenmore Sailing Club, the Weaselhead Flats natural area, and the Earl Grey Golf Club. Southland Leisure Centre is a city-run aquatic and fitness hub close by on Southland Drive. Cedarbrae Community Centre at 11024 Cedarbrae Drive SW hosts active programming including yoga, fitness classes, dance, and year-round community events including Winterfest and the annual Stampede Hoedown.

The November–February slow season is the main softness; July Stampede and summer Spruce Meadows events provide demand spikes. Suite stock is mixed — confirm legality before listing.

renter_intel.title: Southwest Ring Road (Tsuut'ina Trail) — Unlocking Mountain Access
renter_intel.description: The completion of Calgary's Southwest Ring Road (Stoney Trail / Tsuut'ina Trail) transformed Cedarbrae's western boundary into a premium regional highway access point. Guests bound for Kananaskis Country, Bragg Creek, and Banff National Park can exit via this route without navigating central Calgary, cutting drive times materially. This positions Cedarbrae as a genuine mountain gateway community for summer and ski-season guests.

renter_intel.title: Glenmore Reservoir and South Glenmore Park — Walking Distance
renter_intel.description: The Glenmore Reservoir's southern shore runs immediately north of Cedarbrae. South Glenmore Park at 14 Street and 90 Avenue SW provides a full outdoor amenity hub: sailing, kayaking, canoeing, a splash park, tennis courts, and the 16 km reservoir pathway loop. Access to the Glenmore Sailing Club's learn-to-sail programs and boat rentals (May through October) is a meaningful summer differentiator for the right listing.

renter_intel.title: C-Train Access — Heritage and Southland Stations
renter_intel.description: Heritage and Southland C-Train stations on the Red Line are accessible from Cedarbrae in under 10 minutes by car or short bus ride, providing rail access to downtown Calgary in approximately 25 minutes. For car-free corporate guests and event visitors, this transit connectivity meaningfully expands the bookable guest pool beyond those requiring personal vehicle access.

whats_nearby.category: South Glenmore Park (Glenmore Sailing Club, Reservoir Pathway) — 5 min
whats_nearby.items: South Glenmore Park at 8601 24 Street SW is the primary outdoor amenity for Cedarbrae residents. The park fronts the Glenmore Reservoir and hosts the Glenmore Sailing Club (racing, lessons, rentals), a large spray and aquatic playground, tennis courts, and direct access to the 16 km uninterrupted reservoir pathway. Open May through October for water activities.

whats_nearby.category: Spruce Meadows — 15 min via Stoney Trail
whats_nearby.items: Cedarbrae's access to Stoney Trail via Tsuut'ina Trail puts Spruce Meadows' show-jumping complex within a 15-minute drive. The venue's spring through fall tournament calendar (National, Continental, Masters) generates significant accommodation demand in south Calgary communities with no direct hotel cluster nearby.

Cedarbrae sits between two of Calgary's largest south-end retail centres. Southcentre Mall on Anderson Road east of the community provides full department store and dining access. CF Chinook Centre on Macleod Trail at Glenmore Trail is Calgary's largest mall, offering premium retail, dining, and entertainment. Both are within a 10-minute drive from the community core.

cedarbrae.peak_season: June–August (Spruce Meadows proximity, mountain gateway). July Stampede secondary.
cedarbrae.guest_profile: Spruce Meadows event attendees, mountain-bound families, corporate travellers on Macleod Trail.
cedarbrae.stand_out: Detached homes near Macleod Trail with easy Spruce Meadows access.
cedarbrae.distinctive_fact: Cedarbrae's Macleod Trail access makes it a viable option for corporate travellers and Spruce Meadows event attendees. BNBCalc data for this SW zone shows consistent summer performance for well-run whole homes.

schools.name: Tsuu T'ina Bullhead Adult Education Centre
Dr. E. P. Scarlett High School

kelvin-grove.name: Kelvin Grove
kelvin-grove.subtitle: Affluent SW Enclave with Glenmore Reservoir Views and Hospital Proximity
kelvin-grove.str_intro:
Kelvin Grove is a quiet, established residential community in southwest Calgary, bounded by Glenmore Trail to the north, Elbow Drive to the east, 75 Avenue SW to the south, and 14 Street SW to the west. The community was established in 1957 and sits at the eastern edge of the Glenmore Reservoir, with properties on the western side of the community enjoying reservoir views and direct pathway access. Rockyview General Hospital borders the community immediately to the west, making Kelvin Grove a practical base for visiting medical professionals, patient families, and healthcare travellers. Average assessed property values sit at $1,087,840 — 41% above the city-wide average — reflecting the premium character of the area.

Kelvin Grove is covered by the CKE Community Association (Chinook Park, Kelvin Grove, Eagle Ridge), which operates a community facility with a gymnasium, dance studio, outdoor skating rink, tennis courts, and a community garden. The Glenmore Reservoir pathway system provides immediate outdoor access: North Glenmore Park to the west offers 208 acres of trails, tennis courts, fire pits, and reservoir waterfront, while Weaselhead Flats — 585 acres of urban nature preserve with spruce forests and wildlife — is a 10-minute drive. CF Chinook Centre, Calgary's largest shopping mall, is a short drive south on Macleod Trail at Glenmore Trail. Elbow Drive connects the community cleanly to downtown Calgary in approximately 20 minutes.

Kelvin Grove's housing stock is primarily renovated mid-century detached homes on wide lots — average year of construction is 1973 — with many properties having undergone significant contemporary updates. The 583-property community is small and supply-constrained, which supports strong per-night pricing for well-positioned listings. Best STR properties are detached 3-bedroom homes toward the reservoir boundary, which can command views-based premiums. The guest profile skews toward outdoor recreation visitors, hospital-related travel, and families seeking a premium SW location near Chinook Centre. Suite stock is mixed; the CKE Community Association area has higher proportions of single detached homes.
kelvin-grove.str_lifestyle: Kelvin Grove is covered by the CKE Community Association (Chinook Park, Kelvin Grove, Eagle Ridge), which operates a community facility with a gymnasium, dance studio, outdoor skating rink, tennis courts, and a community garden. The Glenmore Reservoir pathway system provides immediate outdoor access: North Glenmore Park to the west offers 208 acres of trails, tennis courts, fire pits, and reservoir waterfront, while Weaselhead Flats — 585 acres of urban nature preserve with spruce forests and wildlife — is a 10-minute drive. CF Chinook Centre, Calgary's largest shopping mall, is a short drive south on Macleod Trail at Glenmore Trail. Elbow Drive connects the community cleanly to downtown Calgary in approximately 20 minutes.

The guest profile skews toward outdoor recreation visitors, hospital-related travel, and families seeking a premium SW location near Chinook Centre. Suite stock is mixed; the CKE Community Association area has higher proportions of single detached homes.

renter_intel.title: Rockyview General Hospital — Immediately Adjacent
renter_intel.description: Rockyview General Hospital, a large acute-care facility administered by Alberta Health Services, sits directly on the western edge of Kelvin Grove. This creates consistent, year-round demand from visiting patient families, medical professionals on temporary assignment, and staff on extended clinical rotations — a guest segment that values whole-home comfort and proximity to the hospital over price sensitivity.

renter_intel.title: Glenmore Reservoir Pathway Access — Walking Distance for Some Properties
renter_intel.description: Properties on the western streets of Kelvin Grove back toward the Glenmore Reservoir pathway system, offering guests direct walking or cycling access to the 16 km reservoir loop. North Glenmore Park at the community's northwest edge provides waterfront access, picnic areas, fire pits, tennis courts, and kayak and canoe rentals (May–October at the Glenmore Sailing School). This is the primary lifestyle differentiator for listing photography.

renter_intel.title: Calgary JCC and Elbow Drive Amenity Corridor — 5 min
renter_intel.description: The Mayfair commercial strip along Elbow Drive provides walkable convenience for Kelvin Grove residents: Mayfair Food Market, Shoppers Drug Mart, a 7-Eleven, and multiple professional services. Calgary JCC (Jewish Community Centre) nearby on Elbow Drive offers an indoor saltwater pool, fitness centre, and gymnasium accessible to non-members via day passes — a useful wet-weather amenity for families.

whats_nearby.items: North Glenmore Park at the north end of 14 Street SW encompasses 208 acres of reservoir-front greenspace with paved and unpaved trails, tennis courts, fire pits and BBQ areas, a children's playground, and direct access to boat launch facilities. The Glenmore Sailing School and Calgary Canoe Club are based here, offering lessons and rentals from May through October.

CF Chinook Centre on Macleod Trail at Glenmore Trail is Calgary's largest enclosed shopping mall, with over 240 stores, multiple dining options, and entertainment anchor tenants. It is the primary retail and dining destination for southwest Calgary residents. The 10-minute drive from Kelvin Grove makes it an easy day-trip anchor for shopping-oriented guests.

Heritage Park Historical Village, located on a peninsula of the Glenmore Reservoir at 1900 Heritage Drive SW, is Canada's largest living history museum. Accessible via the reservoir pathway by bicycle or a short drive, Heritage Park provides a family-friendly half-day activity with operating heritage streetcars, a steam train, midway rides, and working historical exhibits. Open May through early October.

kelvin-grove.peak_season: June–August (Glenmore Reservoir proximity, mountain gateway). July Stampede secondary.
kelvin-grove.guest_profile: Outdoor enthusiasts, mountain-bound families, golf tourists.
kelvin-grove.stand_out: Detached homes near the Glenmore Reservoir with pathway access.
kelvin-grove.distinctive_fact: Kelvin Grove borders the Glenmore Reservoir — properties with reservoir views or pathway access see stronger bookings from outdoor recreation guests. BNBCalc data for this SW zone shows consistent summer performance.

maple-ridge.name: Maple Ridge
maple-ridge.subtitle: Quiet SE Calgary with City-Owned Golf Course and Reservoir Pathway Access
maple-ridge.str_intro:
Maple Ridge is a mature residential community in southeast Calgary, bounded by Deerfoot Trail to the east, Southland Drive to the north, Anderson Road to the south, and the community of Willow Park to the west. The community is defined by the Maple Ridge Golf Course, an 18-hole, par-72 City of Calgary public facility at 1240 Mapleglade Drive SE — one of the best-value public courses in the city. The golf course lines the community's south and east boundaries, providing a significant greenspace buffer and a lifestyle marketing anchor for STR listings. Anderson Road provides direct east access to Deerfoot Trail and rapid north-south movement across the city.

Maple Ridge and the adjacent Willow Park community operate under the WillowRidge Community Association, providing coordinated programming, outdoor rinks, and green space maintenance. Maple Ridge Golf Course (open approximately May through October) draws golfers from across the city and hosts Tuesday Men's Nights and Wednesday Ladies' Nights throughout the season. Southcentre Mall on Anderson Road is minutes away, alongside the Avenida Food Hall and Fresh Market — a popular artisan food destination. Fish Creek Provincial Park, one of North America's largest urban parks, is accessible 2–3 km to the south, providing extensive hiking, cycling, and river access. The Bow River and Carburn Park are also within close reach.

Maple Ridge's housing stock is predominantly 1970s–1980s single-detached homes, with the community sharing the WillowRidge association footprint alongside the Willow Park golf corridor to the west. Best STR inventory is detached 3-bedroom homes near Mapleglade Drive with golf course adjacency or proximity to the Anderson Road corridor for easy Southcentre and Deerfoot Trail access. Guest profile includes golf tourists (strong spring–fall demand), families visiting south Calgary, and corporate travellers using the Deerfoot Trail for north-south commuting. The golf course's seasonal operation (closed October–April) means summer peak is pronounced; the November–February window is the softest period.

Maple Ridge Golf Course (open approximately May through October) draws golfers from across the city and hosts Tuesday Men's Nights and Wednesday Ladies' Nights throughout the season. Southcentre Mall on Anderson Road is minutes away, alongside the Avenida Food Hall and Fresh Market — a popular artisan food destination. The golf course's seasonal operation (closed October–April) means summer peak is pronounced; the November–February window is the softest period.

renter_intel.title: Maple Ridge Golf Course — City-Owned Public 18-Hole Course on the Doorstep
renter_intel.description: Maple Ridge Golf Course (1240 Mapleglade Drive SE) is a City of Calgary-operated 18-hole, par-72 course open approximately May through October, with open tee-time access for all skill levels and reasonable green fees. Properties adjacent or near the course have direct walking access and a strong golf-tourism marketing angle. The course is categorised as public, meaning guests do not need club memberships.

renter_intel.title: Anderson Road / Deerfoot Trail Connectivity
renter_intel.description: Anderson Road runs along the southern boundary, connecting directly to Deerfoot Trail (Highway 2) for rapid north-south movement across Calgary and south toward Okotoks and Lethbridge. This arterial access makes Maple Ridge a viable base for corporate travellers whose work sites are distributed across the city's south industrial and commercial corridors. Anderson LRT station is also accessible within a 5–10 minute drive.

renter_intel.title: Fish Creek Provincial Park — 5 min South
renter_intel.description: Fish Creek Provincial Park's northern entrance near the Bow River is within a 5-minute drive of Maple Ridge. With 80+ km of trails, cycling paths, picnic sites, and the natural Bow River corridor, Fish Creek is a major non-golf outdoor amenity that extends the community's appeal beyond the golf season into the shoulder months of May and September–October.

whats_nearby.items: The City of Calgary's Maple Ridge Golf Course (1240 Mapleglade Drive SE) is a challenging 6,576-yard, par-72 course with a practice putting green and licensed snack bar. Seasonal green fees are among the most competitive for an 18-hole layout in Calgary. Reservations can be made online or by phone through the City of Calgary recreation booking system.

Southcentre Mall at Macleod Trail and Anderson Road is a full-service enclosed shopping centre with major department stores, a food court, and surrounding big-box retail. The Avenida Food Hall and Fresh Market adjacent to the site is a favourite among food-oriented visitors, with artisan vendors, a Saturday market, and prepared food stalls.

Carburn Park on the Bow River east of the community offers off-leash dog access, riverside walking trails, and peaceful river views. The Bow River pathway connects north and south through much of Calgary's southeast, and Carburn Park is a popular cycling and walking access point for the broader river trail system — appealing to active guests staying in Maple Ridge.

maple-ridge.peak_season: June–August (Glenmore Reservoir proximity, mountain gateway). July Stampede secondary.
maple-ridge.guest_profile: Outdoor enthusiasts, mountain-bound families, golf tourists (Maple Ridge Golf Course).
maple-ridge.stand_out: Detached homes near the Glenmore Reservoir or Maple Ridge Golf Course.
maple-ridge.distinctive_fact: Maple Ridge is adjacent to the Glenmore Reservoir and Maple Ridge Golf Course — properties that market both the reservoir access and the golf proximity see stronger bookings from outdoor recreation guests.

groceries.name: Calgary Co-op Quarry Park Food Centre

oakridge.name: Oakridge
oakridge.subtitle: Southwest Reservoir-Side Community with Weaselhead Nature Reserve Access
oakridge.str_intro:
Oakridge is a mature southwest Calgary community established in 1968, occupying the southwest quadrant between 90 Avenue SW to the north (where it borders the Glenmore Reservoir and Weaselhead Natural Area), 24 Street SW to the east, Southland Drive to the south, and Stoney Trail to the west. The Glenmore Reservoir and its associated Weaselhead Flats Natural Environment Park form Oakridge's northern boundary — one of Calgary's most significant urban nature corridors, spanning 585 acres of spruce forest, wetlands, and wildlife habitat. Stoney Trail on the western edge provides immediate ring-road access to the mountains and Spruce Meadows, establishing Oakridge as a genuine southwest gateway community.

The community's proximity to South Glenmore Park (a 5-minute drive north) and the Weaselhead Flats gives outdoor-oriented guests direct access to the 16 km reservoir pathway loop, Glenmore Sailing Club, Calgary Canoe Club, and wilderness hiking without leaving the city. A Costco on the Tsuut'ina Nation (accessible via Stoney Trail) and Southcentre Mall are within 5–10 minutes, meeting grocery and retail needs comprehensively. Oakridge Community Association at 9504 Oakfield Drive SW maintains active programming, a community rink, and sports fields. The Oakridge Crossing redevelopment — a major mixed-use project replacing the existing Co-op centre — is underway and will add significant new retail and residential density to the community core by the mid-2020s.

Oakridge's housing stock is primarily 1970s single-detached homes with 56% single detached. Average home prices in the $890K–$1.15M range for detached listings reflect the community's proximity to the reservoir and established southwest infrastructure. The STR sweet spot is detached 3-bedroom homes on the northern edge of the community near the reservoir pathway entry points, which provide the strongest lifestyle marketing story. Guest profile: outdoor recreation visitors, mountain-bound families, and golf tourists using the neighbouring Glenmore-area courses. Southland Leisure Centre is close by for indoor wet-weather activities.
oakridge.str_lifestyle: The community's proximity to South Glenmore Park (a 5-minute drive north) and the Weaselhead Flats gives outdoor-oriented guests direct access to the 16 km reservoir pathway loop, Glenmore Sailing Club, Calgary Canoe Club, and wilderness hiking without leaving the city. A Costco on the Tsuut'ina Nation (accessible via Stoney Trail) and Southcentre Mall are within 5–10 minutes, meeting grocery and retail needs comprehensively. Oakridge Community Association at 9504 Oakfield Drive SW maintains active programming, a community rink, and sports fields. The Oakridge Crossing redevelopment — a major mixed-use project replacing the existing Co-op centre — is underway and will add significant new retail and residential density to the community core by the mid-2020s.

renter_intel.title: Weaselhead Flats / Glenmore Reservoir — Footsteps from Some Properties
renter_intel.description: The Weaselhead Natural Environment Park at Oakridge's northern boundary is a 585-acre wilderness park within Calgary city limits, featuring spruce forest, beaver ponds, coyote and deer sightings, and an extensive trail network. Properties on Oakridge's northern streets can access the Weaselhead directly on foot. Combined with the 16 km Glenmore Reservoir pathway, this creates an outstanding urban nature story for outdoor recreation guests.

renter_intel.title: Stoney Trail Ring Road — West Entrance at the Community Edge
renter_intel.description: Stoney Trail SW borders Oakridge to the west, providing direct, traffic-free access to the mountain corridor (Kananaskis Country, Banff, Canmore), the Tsuut'ina Nation retail area (Costco, IKEA), and Spruce Meadows to the south. This ring-road access makes Oakridge one of the most logistically convenient SW communities for guests using Calgary as a Rockies base camp.

renter_intel.title: Glenmore Sailing Club / Sailing School — 5 min
renter_intel.description: The Glenmore Sailing Club at 8601 24 Street SW (South Glenmore Park, adjacent to Oakridge's eastern boundary) offers sailing lessons, Wednesday and Sunday racing, and social events from May through mid-October. The City of Calgary's Glenmore Sailing School provides kayak and canoe rentals from June through Labour Day. These water activities are a distinctive summer amenity for active guests.

whats_nearby.category: Weaselhead Flats Natural Environment Park — Walking Distance
whats_nearby.items: Weaselhead Flats is a 585-acre protected natural area at the west end of the Glenmore Reservoir. The park is accessed from Oakridge's northern boundary and contains multi-use dirt and gravel trails through dense spruce and poplar forest, crossing multiple streams and wetlands. Wildlife sightings — deer, beavers, great blue herons, and various raptors — are common. Open year-round including winter cross-country skiing.

whats_nearby.category: South Glenmore Park and Reservoir Pathway — 5 min
whats_nearby.items: South Glenmore Park at 14 Street and 90 Avenue SW provides the south anchor of the 16 km Glenmore Reservoir pathway loop. The park contains a spray park and aquatic playground, tennis courts, bike track, the Glenmore Sailing Club, Heritage Marina beach, and kayak and canoe rentals. Heritage Park Historical Village is a short cycle north along the pathway from here.

Southcentre Mall on Anderson Road provides full retail and dining access. The Costco on the Tsuut'ina Nation (accessible via Stoney Trail west of Oakridge) provides large-format grocery and bulk shopping convenient for extended family stays. Both are within a 5–10 minute drive from the community core.

oakridge.peak_season: June–August (Glenmore Reservoir proximity, mountain gateway). July Stampede secondary.
oakridge.guest_profile: Outdoor enthusiasts, mountain-bound families, golf tourists.
oakridge.stand_out: Detached homes near the Glenmore Reservoir with pathway access.
oakridge.distinctive_fact: Oakridge borders the Glenmore Reservoir — properties with reservoir views or pathway access see stronger bookings from outdoor recreation guests. BNBCalc data for this SW zone shows consistent summer performance.

schools.name: Tsuu T'ina Bullhead Adult Education Centre

palliser.name: Palliser
palliser.subtitle: Established SW Calgary with Southland Leisure Centre and Reservoir Pathway Access
palliser.str_intro:
Palliser is a settled southwest Calgary community, developed from the 1960s onward and bounded by Southland Drive and 90 Avenue to the north, 19 Street SW to the east, and 24 Street SW to the west. South Glenmore Park sits directly across from the community's northwest corner, providing immediate access to the Glenmore Reservoir and its 16 km multi-use pathway system — including boat docks, the Weaselhead Flats hiking corridor, and Heritage Park Historical Village. The community is served by the Palliser-Bayview-Pumphill Community Association at 2323 Palliser Drive SW. Macleod Trail is accessible within minutes to the east, providing direct arterial connectivity to downtown and the south corridor.

Southland Leisure Centre, located within the community's area on Southland Drive SW, is a City of Calgary flagship recreation facility offering an indoor wave pool, fitness centre, skating rink, and extensive programming year-round — a substantial wet-weather and winter amenity for families. The pathway from South Glenmore Park connects Palliser residents to Heritage Park Historical Village by bicycle along the reservoir's southern shore. Palliser has two elementary schools and one junior high school within its boundaries, making it a functional family destination. Glenmore Landing (a major commercial and professional services hub at 14 Street and Glenmore Trail) provides convenient grocery and retail access.

Palliser's housing stock is predominantly 1970s single-detached homes with a community density typical of Calgary's suburban boom era. Best STR inventory is detached 3-bedroom homes on quieter residential streets, particularly those with walking access toward South Glenmore Park. The guest profile skews toward outdoor recreation visitors, mountain-bound families, and Spruce Meadows event attendees — all of whom benefit from Palliser's south and ring-road access via Stoney Trail. The November–February window is the softest demand period; July Stampede and summer Spruce Meadows tournaments provide booking spikes.
palliser.str_lifestyle: Southland Leisure Centre, located within the community's area on Southland Drive SW, is a City of Calgary flagship recreation facility offering an indoor wave pool, fitness centre, skating rink, and extensive programming year-round — a substantial wet-weather and winter amenity for families. The pathway from South Glenmore Park connects Palliser residents to Heritage Park Historical Village by bicycle along the reservoir's southern shore. Palliser has two elementary schools and one junior high school within its boundaries, making it a functional family destination. Glenmore Landing (a major commercial and professional services hub at 14 Street and Glenmore Trail) provides convenient grocery and retail access.

The November–February window is the softest demand period; July Stampede and summer Spruce Meadows tournaments provide booking spikes.

renter_intel.title: Southland Leisure Centre — In the Community Area
renter_intel.description: Southland Leisure Centre on Southland Drive SW is one of Calgary's premier City-operated recreation facilities, housing an indoor wave pool and aquatic playground, twin NHL ice surfaces, a fitness centre, and multipurpose programming spaces. For families with children, this is a year-round anchor activity that eliminates the need to drive across the city for indoor recreation. Open daily with public admission.

renter_intel.title: South Glenmore Park / Reservoir Pathway — Walking Distance from North Palliser
renter_intel.description: The northern edge of Palliser is linked to South Glenmore Park via the pathway system, providing access to the 16 km Glenmore Reservoir loop. Properties on the north side of the community near 90 Avenue SW can market direct pathway access as a premium feature. The park hosts the Glenmore Sailing Club, Heritage Marina, and the full suite of water sport amenities from May through October.

renter_intel.title: Heritage Park Historical Village — Accessible by Pathway
renter_intel.description: Heritage Park Historical Village — Canada's largest living history museum, located on a Glenmore Reservoir peninsula — is reachable by bicycle along the reservoir pathway from Palliser's north end. Families can cycle to Heritage Park for a half-day visit without a car, a distinctive selling point for family groups. Admission and seasons should be confirmed at heritagepark.ca.

whats_nearby.category: South Glenmore Park and Heritage Park — 5 min or Cycle
whats_nearby.items: South Glenmore Park's splash park, Glenmore Sailing Club, and pathway access are 5 minutes north by car from Palliser. Heritage Park Historical Village is accessible by the same reservoir pathway, or by a 5-minute drive. Together these two venues make the community's northern access a genuine day-trip hub for visiting families.

whats_nearby.category: Southcentre Mall and Macleod Trail Corridor — 5 min
whats_nearby.items: Southcentre Mall on Anderson Road east of Palliser provides full retail and dining access, including major department stores, restaurants, and a cinema. Macleod Trail's full south corridor of restaurants, car dealers, and commercial services is accessible within 5 minutes. Guests can meet all provisioning needs within a short drive from the property.

whats_nearby.category: Spruce Meadows — 15–20 min via Stoney Trail
whats_nearby.items: Palliser's proximity to Stoney Trail's southwest interchange puts Spruce Meadows within a 15–20 minute drive. The venue's spring through fall international show-jumping calendar draws visitors from across Canada and internationally, creating periodic accommodation demand spikes in communities without local hotel inventory. Palliser's whole-home inventory is well-positioned for this audience.

palliser.guest_profile: Outdoor enthusiasts, mountain-bound families, golf tourists.
palliser.stand_out: Detached homes near the Glenmore Reservoir with pathway access.

willow-park.name: Willow Park
willow-park.subtitle: Tree-Lined SE Calgary Neighbourhood Defined by a Private Golf and Country Club
willow-park.str_intro:
Willow Park is an established, prestigious southeast Calgary community, bounded by Macleod Trail to the west, Anderson Road to the south, Southland Drive to the north, and the community of Maple Ridge to the east. The Willow Park Golf & Country Club — a private 18-hole, par-71 course established in 1965 at 639 Willow Park Drive SE — bisects the community from west to east, functioning as both its defining green corridor and its strongest lifestyle marketing anchor. Golf Canada lists the course at 6,439 yards from the blue tees with a slope of 130, placing it among Calgary's more demanding private layouts. Southcentre Mall and Willow Park Village (a boutique retail and dining strip) are located in the southwest corner of the community on Macleod Trail.

Willow Park is among Calgary's most coveted established SE communities, characterised by tree-lined streets on wide lots, spacious mid-century homes, and a well-maintained residential streetscape. Willow Park Village provides walkable boutique shopping, specialty food stores, and local services. Fairmont Drive bisects the community north to south, offering quiet residential character away from the Macleod Trail commercial corridor. Fish Creek Provincial Park borders the southern outskirts via Anderson Road, extending the outdoor recreation options into hiking, cycling, and the Bow River natural area. The community is served by the WillowRidge Community Association jointly with Maple Ridge.

Willow Park's housing stock is predominantly established single-detached homes on larger lots — a mix of 1970s construction and extensively renovated properties. Proximity to the private golf course commands a consistent premium. Best STR inventory is detached 3-bedroom homes near the golf course corridor or Macleod Trail, targeting golf tourists accessing Willow Park Golf & Country Club, corporate travellers on the Macleod Trail corridor, and families visiting south Calgary. Being a private club, guests cannot directly access the Willow Park golf course without membership — hosts should note this accurately in listings. The November–February slow season is the main demand softness.
willow-park.str_lifestyle: Willow Park is among Calgary's most coveted established SE communities, characterised by tree-lined streets on wide lots, spacious mid-century homes, and a well-maintained residential streetscape. Willow Park Village provides walkable boutique shopping, specialty food stores, and local services. Fairmont Drive bisects the community north to south, offering quiet residential character away from the Macleod Trail commercial corridor. Fish Creek Provincial Park borders the southern outskirts via Anderson Road, extending the outdoor recreation options into hiking, cycling, and the Bow River natural area. The community is served by the WillowRidge Community Association jointly with Maple Ridge.

Being a private club, guests cannot directly access the Willow Park golf course without membership — hosts should note this accurately in listings. The November–February slow season is the main demand softness.

renter_intel.title: Willow Park Golf & Country Club — Defining Community Amenity
renter_intel.description: Willow Park Golf & Country Club (639 Willow Park Drive SE, 403-271-4231) is a private 18-hole par-71 course established in 1965, considered one of Calgary's finest short game facilities per the club's own marketing. Membership is required for play — the club is not publicly accessible. STR properties near the course benefit from the premium greenspace and course views in listing photography; guests seeking golf access should be directed to public courses such as Maple Ridge or McCall Lake Golf Course.

renter_intel.title: Willow Park Village — Walkable Retail and Dining
renter_intel.description: Willow Park Village at the southwest corner of the community on Macleod Trail provides a walkable retail and dining strip with boutique specialty food purveyors, local services, and restaurants. For extended-stay guests who value neighbourhood character over big-box convenience, the Village is a premium local amenity. Southcentre Mall is directly adjacent for full retail coverage.

renter_intel.title: Anderson C-Train / Southland C-Train — 5–10 min
renter_intel.description: Both Anderson and Southland LRT stations on the Red Line are within 5–10 minutes of Willow Park, providing downtown Calgary access in approximately 25–30 minutes without a car. For corporate travellers and conference guests staying in the community, this rail option meaningfully reduces the need for a rental vehicle — a booking conversion factor for cost-conscious guests.

whats_nearby.category: Willow Park Golf & Country Club — In the Community
whats_nearby.items: The 18-hole, par-71 Willow Park Golf & Country Club is located at 639 Willow Park Drive SE and runs through the heart of the community. A private members' club established in 1965, it offers one of the finest short game facilities in western Canada with a full practice facility, pro shop, and licensed restaurant. Private for members; confirm access before marketing to golf guest segments.

whats_nearby.category: Southcentre Mall / Anderson Road Retail — 3 min
whats_nearby.items: Southcentre Mall at Macleod Trail and Anderson Road is within a 3-minute drive of Willow Park, providing the full Calgary south-end retail and dining offering: major department stores, restaurant chains, Cineplex cinema, and the Avenida Food Hall and Fresh Market for artisan grocery and prepared food. It is the community's primary provisioning hub for out-of-town guests.

Fish Creek Provincial Park's northern access points are within 10 minutes of Willow Park via Anderson Road. One of North America's largest urban parks, Fish Creek contains over 80 km of multi-use trails, cycling paths, the Bow River corridor, Sikome Lake (a supervised swimming lake), and multiple picnic and nature immersion sites — a major year-round outdoor amenity for active guests.

willow-park.peak_season: June–August (Willow Park Golf Course, Macleod Trail access). July Stampede secondary.
willow-park.best_property_type: Detached 3-bed near Willow Park Golf Course or Macleod Trail.
willow-park.guest_profile: Golf tourists, corporate travellers, families visiting south Calgary.
willow-park.stand_out: Detached homes near Willow Park Golf Course or Macleod Trail.
willow-park.distinctive_fact: Willow Park is home to the Willow Park Golf Course — properties near the course can market the golf access to golf tourists. BNBCalc data for this south zone shows consistent performance for well-run whole homes.

schools.name: Dr. E. P. Scarlett High School

bayview.name: Bayview
bayview.subtitle: Ultra-Exclusive Glenmore Reservoir Peninsula — Calgary's Premier Waterfront Address
bayview.str_intro:
Bayview is one of Calgary's smallest and most exclusive residential communities, occupying a peninsula on the southern shore of the Glenmore Reservoir in the southwest quadrant. Established in 1967, the community contains approximately 249 single-family detached homes — all on the Glenmore Reservoir's shoreline or within steps of it — and is home to one of the reservoir's two marinas. With a median household income of $296,000 (one of the highest in the city) and 98% owner-occupied housing, Bayview is a tight-supply, premium market where STR availability is inherently limited. Heritage Drive, Crowchild Trail, and Macleod Trail provide vehicular access to downtown Calgary in approximately 20 minutes.

Bayview's defining characteristic is immediate reservoir waterfront access. Residents and guests can walk to the marina, launch kayaks and canoes, and access the 16 km Glenmore Reservoir pathway system that connects to Heritage Park Historical Village, South Glenmore Park, the Weaselhead Flats, and the Glenmore Sailing Club — all without a vehicle. Adjacent South Glenmore Park at the community's eastern edge provides a splash park, tennis courts, and the Glenmore Sailing School's public lessons and rentals. Glenmore Landing shopping centre (14 Street and Glenmore Trail) provides immediate grocery, restaurant, and professional services access. CF Chinook Centre is a 5-minute drive south on Macleod Trail.

Bayview's housing stock is entirely single-detached, ranging from original 1960s–1970s homes with reservoir views to substantially redeveloped or rebuilt luxury residences. Given the community's extremely small size and near-universal owner-occupancy, STR opportunities arise rarely and command a significant premium when available. A well-presented 3-bedroom detached home with reservoir views or marina proximity in Bayview can position at the top of the southwest nightly rate range. Investors should be aware that acquisition prices reflect the community's premium status — entry-point cost modelling is essential before committing to STR strategy here.
bayview.str_lifestyle: Bayview's defining characteristic is immediate reservoir waterfront access. Residents and guests can walk to the marina, launch kayaks and canoes, and access the 16 km Glenmore Reservoir pathway system that connects to Heritage Park Historical Village, South Glenmore Park, the Weaselhead Flats, and the Glenmore Sailing Club — all without a vehicle. Adjacent South Glenmore Park at the community's eastern edge provides a splash park, tennis courts, and the Glenmore Sailing School's public lessons and rentals. Glenmore Landing shopping centre (14 Street and Glenmore Trail) provides immediate grocery, restaurant, and professional services access. CF Chinook Centre is a 5-minute drive south on Macleod Trail.

Given the community's extremely small size and near-universal owner-occupancy, STR opportunities arise rarely and command a significant premium when available. A well-presented 3-bedroom detached home with reservoir views or marina proximity in Bayview can position at the top of the southwest nightly rate range. Investors should be aware that acquisition prices reflect the community's premium status — entry-point cost modelling is essential before committing to STR strategy here.

renter_intel.title: Marina and Reservoir Waterfront — Calgary's Finest Urban Lakefront Setting
renter_intel.description: Bayview's marina is one of only two on the Glenmore Reservoir. Guests staying in Bayview properties with marina access or reservoir views experience a genuinely unusual urban waterfront stay — unusual by any Canadian city standard, let alone Calgary. The marina and reservoir are open for sailing, kayaking, canoeing, and fishing from May 1 through October 31. This is the primary premium differentiator for Bayview STR listings.

renter_intel.title: 16 km Glenmore Reservoir Pathway — Direct Access
renter_intel.description: The 16 km Glenmore Reservoir multi-use pathway system is accessible on foot from Bayview properties, connecting to Heritage Park Historical Village to the west, South Glenmore Park to the east, the Glenmore Sailing Club, Calgary Rowing Club, Calgary Canoe Club, and the Earl Grey Golf Club. For cycling guests, this pathway provides an entire day's worth of exploration without crossing a road.

renter_intel.title: Supply Constraint Drives Premium Pricing
renter_intel.description: With only approximately 249 dwellings in the entire community and near-universal owner-occupancy, STR inventory in Bayview is inherently scarce. Supply constraint supports premium nightly rates for operators who establish a well-managed listing. The upper end of the $145–$255 nightly range should be achievable for well-presented reservoir-adjacent properties — with scope to exceed it during peak summer weekends.

whats_nearby.category: Glenmore Reservoir Marina — In the Community
whats_nearby.items: One of the Glenmore Reservoir's two marinas is located within Bayview, providing boat mooring, fishing access, and a launching point for non-motorised watercraft. The reservoir is open May 1 through October 31 for sailing, rowing, canoeing, and fishing. Swimming is not permitted in the reservoir (it is a drinking water source).

whats_nearby.category: Heritage Park Historical Village — 10 min or Cycle
whats_nearby.items: Heritage Park Historical Village at 1900 Heritage Drive SW is accessible by car in 10 minutes or by bicycle via the reservoir pathway's western arm. Canada's largest living history museum occupies a Glenmore Reservoir peninsula with operating steam train, heritage streetcars, midway rides, and working historical exhibits. Open May through early October.

whats_nearby.category: CF Chinook Centre and Glenmore Landing — 5–8 min
whats_nearby.items: Glenmore Landing shopping centre at 14 Street and Glenmore Trail provides immediate daily provisioning: grocery, restaurant, professional services, and banking directly adjacent to the community. CF Chinook Centre — Calgary's largest enclosed mall — is a 5-minute drive south on Macleod Trail for major retail, dining, and entertainment.

bayview.peak_season: June–August (Glenmore Reservoir proximity, mountain gateway). July Stampede secondary.
bayview.stand_out: Detached homes near the Glenmore Reservoir with pathway access.
bayview.distinctive_fact: Bayview borders the Glenmore Reservoir — properties with reservoir views or pathway access see stronger bookings from outdoor recreation guests. BNBCalc data for this SW zone shows consistent summer performance.

coral-springs.name: Coral Springs
coral-springs.subtitle: NE Calgary Lake Community with Private Beach and Airport Proximity
coral-springs.str_intro:
Coral Springs is a distinctive lake community in Calgary's northeast quadrant, established in 1991 with a Californian architectural theme and a private 16-acre lake at its centre. The community is bounded by 64 Avenue NE to the north, Stoney Trail to the east, McKnight Boulevard to the south, and 68 Street NE to the west — a location that delivers meaningful airport proximity (Calgary International Airport is approximately 15–20 minutes via McKnight Boulevard and Barlow Trail) alongside the year-round family appeal of a private urban lake. Lake access privileges are managed by the Coral Springs Residents Association, and properties with active lake membership qualifications are the primary STR demand driver.

The Coral Springs lake experience is unusually complete for an urban community: a private beach, beach volleyball court, fishing access stocked with rainbow trout from the lake's private hatchery, recreational boating, and in winter, outdoor ice skating on the lake surface. The Don Hartman Northeast Sportsplex — located in the community — provides two NHL-sized hockey arenas, a gymnasium for basketball and volleyball, and a fitness training room. Community events are a genuine draw: an annual Stampede party, Winterfest in February, a family corn roast in September, and a Halloween party in October. Calgary Transit bus routes serve McKnight Boulevard, and Stoney Trail provides east-west ring-road connectivity.

Coral Springs' STR appeal is narrowly focused on detached 3- to 4-bedroom homes with lake access privileges — the community association membership status is the decisive listing qualifier. Properties without active lake access should not be marketed on the lake angle. Separate entry suite stock is rated 'Rare', meaning whole-home detached is the dominant format. Summer demand peaks strongly from June through August; year-round secondary demand comes from airport proximity. The November–March slow season is the softest window. Verify that the specific property qualifies for lake access through the Residents Association before listing lake access as a feature.
coral-springs.str_lifestyle: The Coral Springs lake experience is unusually complete for an urban community: a private beach, beach volleyball court, fishing access stocked with rainbow trout from the lake's private hatchery, recreational boating, and in winter, outdoor ice skating on the lake surface. The Don Hartman Northeast Sportsplex — located in the community — provides two NHL-sized hockey arenas, a gymnasium for basketball and volleyball, and a fitness training room. Community events are a genuine draw: an annual Stampede party, Winterfest in February, a family corn roast in September, and a Halloween party in October. Calgary Transit bus routes serve McKnight Boulevard, and Stoney Trail provides east-west ring-road connectivity.
